name: Stable trial_id primary key (M3)
description: Add trial_id = sha256(task_id || config_name || started_at || model)[:32] and trial_id_full (64 hex) to TrialSignals. Compute during signal extraction in signals.py. Add both fields to TrialSignals TypedDict in types.py. Include the 7 property tests from the PRD spec plus the real-corpus collision test.
acceptance: TrialSignals TypedDict in types.py declares 'trial_id: str' and 'trial_id_full: str'
extract_signals() in signals.py computes trial_id from sha256(task_id||config_name||started_at||model)[:32] using '||' literal separator
trial_id_full is the full 64-hex sha256, trial_id is the first 32 hex characters
tests/test_trial_id.py contains all 7 property tests (P1-P7) from the PRD spec and they pass
pytest tests/test_trial_id.py passes with 0 failures
write_jsonl() output includes trial_id and trial_id_full in each row

name: Models identity mapping + dependency pins (M0 prereq, R4/D5)
description: Create src/agent_diagnostics/models.yaml mapping logical annotator identities (e.g. 'llm:haiku-4') to Anthropic snapshot model IDs. Add a load_models_config() helper in a new src/agent_diagnostics/model_identity.py that loads models.yaml and provides resolve_identity(snapshot_id) -> logical identity and resolve_snapshot(logical_id) -> snapshot_id. Pin duckdb>=1.0,<2.0 and pyarrow>=15.0,<18.0 in pyproject.toml[project.optional-dependencies] under a new 'query' extra. Add tests for model identity resolution.
acceptance: src/agent_diagnostics/models.yaml exists with mappings for llm:haiku-4, llm:sonnet-4, llm:opus-4 to current Anthropic snapshot IDs
src/agent_diagnostics/model_identity.py exports load_models_config() and resolve_identity(snapshot_id) -> logical_identity
resolve_identity('claude-haiku-4-5-20251001') returns 'llm:haiku-4'
resolve_identity with an unknown snapshot ID raises a clear ValueError
pyproject.toml has a 'query' optional-dependency group with duckdb and pyarrow pins
pytest tests/test_model_identity.py passes with 0 failures

name: AnnotationStore write contract (M0 core)
description: Create src/agent_diagnostics/annotation_store.py implementing the AnnotationStore class with narrow-tall JSONL schema. The store must: (a) enforce PK uniqueness (trial_id, category_name, annotator_type, annotator_identity, taxonomy_version) per upsert_annotations() call; (b) append/merge to annotations.jsonl in narrow-tall schema (columns: trial_id, category_name, confidence, evidence, annotator_type, annotator_identity, taxonomy_version, annotated_at); (c) write atomically via temp-file + os.rename; (d) hold advisory lock (fcntl.flock on Unix) across read-merge-write with documented cross-platform note; (e) refuse mixed-version writes — if incoming taxonomy_version or schema_version differs from existing, raise MixedVersionError; (f) resolve annotator_identity via model_identity.resolve_identity(). Readers dedupe last-writer-wins by annotated_at.
acceptance: src/agent_diagnostics/annotation_store.py exports AnnotationStore class and MixedVersionError exception
upsert_annotations() enforces PK uniqueness — duplicate PK raises clear error naming the offending key
Writing taxonomy_version='v4' to a file with existing 'v3' rows raises MixedVersionError naming both versions
Writes use atomic temp-file + os.rename pattern
Advisory file locking via fcntl.flock on Unix with documented Windows note
annotator_identity is resolved via model_identity — raw snapshot IDs never stored in JSONL
read_annotations() returns deduplicated rows (last-writer-wins by annotated_at)
pytest tests/test_annotation_store.py passes with 0 failures

name: DuckDB query subcommand (M1)
description: Create src/agent_diagnostics/query.py with run_query(sql, data_dir) that: auto-registers 'signals', 'annotations', 'manifests' table aliases from JSONL or Parquet files in data_dir (prefer Parquet in data_dir/export/ if present); runs SQL via DuckDB; returns results as list of dicts. Add 'observatory query "SELECT ..."' subcommand to cli.py with --data-dir (default 'data/'). Print results as formatted table. Include tests with small fixture JSONL files. Note: cli.py already has subcommand pattern via argparse — add the 'query' subcommand to main() and a new cmd_query() function.
acceptance: src/agent_diagnostics/query.py exports run_query(sql, data_dir) function
Auto-registers 'signals', 'annotations', 'manifests' table aliases from JSONL or Parquet
Prefers Parquet files when both JSONL and Parquet exist
observatory query 'SELECT count(*) as n FROM signals' works against test fixture JSONL
cli.py has 'query' subcommand via cmd_query() with --data-dir option
Only requires duckdb as runtime dependency (already pinned in query extra)
pytest tests/test_query.py passes with 0 failures

name: Refactor annotation writers to use AnnotationStore (M0 writers)
description: Refactor the four annotation writers to write through AnnotationStore.upsert_annotations() instead of raw json.dump. Each writer converts output to narrow-tall schema before calling upsert. Writers: (1) cmd_annotate -> annotator_type='heuristic', annotator_identity='heuristic:rule-engine'; (2) cmd_llm_annotate -> annotator_type='llm', identity resolved from model arg via models.yaml; (3) cmd_predict -> annotator_type='classifier'; (4) cmd_ensemble/ensemble_all -> annotator_type='ensemble'. ensemble_all should read via AnnotationStore.read_annotations() not its own merge loop. Keep ensemble_all as public API in __init__.py. Add --annotations-out arg (default data/annotations.jsonl) to each command. Add integration test: run all 4 writers sequentially on 20-trial fixture, verify zero duplicate PKs in output.
acceptance: cmd_annotate writes via AnnotationStore with annotator_type='heuristic'
cmd_llm_annotate writes via AnnotationStore with annotator_type='llm'
cmd_predict writes via AnnotationStore with annotator_type='classifier'
cmd_ensemble/ensemble_all writes via AnnotationStore with annotator_type='ensemble'
ensemble_all remains exported from __init__.py (R9 backwards compat)
Running all 4 writers sequentially on test fixture produces zero duplicate PKs
pytest tests/test_writer_refactor.py passes with 0 failures

name: Parquet export + manifest (M4, M5)
description: Create src/agent_diagnostics/export.py with export_parquet(data_dir, out_dir) that reads signals.jsonl and annotations.jsonl from data_dir, writes signals.parquet (native list<string> columns, zstd compression), annotations.parquet, manifests.parquet to out_dir, plus MANIFEST.json with {schema_version, taxonomy_version, row_count, sha256_per_file, source_commit, generated_at}. Compute version via SELECT DISTINCT (R6) and fail if >1 version found. Add 'observatory export --format parquet --out data/export/' subcommand to cli.py. Add 'observatory manifest refresh' that rewrites manifests.jsonl only. Commit MANIFEST.json schema at src/agent_diagnostics/export_manifest_schema.json. Re-running on unchanged inputs must produce byte-identical Parquet except generated_at in MANIFEST. Use sorted row order for determinism. Include tests.
acceptance: src/agent_diagnostics/export.py exports export_parquet(data_dir, out_dir)
signals.parquet has native list<string> columns (tool_call_sequence, files_read_list, files_edited_list)
Parquet files use zstd compression
MANIFEST.json has schema_version, taxonomy_version, row_count, sha256_per_file, source_commit, generated_at
MANIFEST.json validates against export_manifest_schema.json
pandas.read_parquet on signals.parquet works with only pandas+pyarrow
Re-running on unchanged inputs produces byte-identical Parquet
observatory export and observatory manifest refresh subcommands work
pytest tests/test_export.py passes with 0 failures

name: 5 target queries + schema introspection (S4, S2)
description: Commit 5 SQL queries to docs/queries/: (1) per_model_outcomes.sql, (2) benchmark_model_matrix.sql, (3) annotation_cooccurrence.sql, (4) tool_sequence_patterns.sql, (5) eval_subset_export.sql. Each runs via run_query(). Add 'observatory db schema [--format json|markdown]' subcommand that emits schema of signals/annotations/manifests (reads pyarrow schema for Parquet, DuckDB inferred for JSONL). Add pytest tests verifying each query runs and returns expected column names.
acceptance: docs/queries/ has 5 SQL files covering the 5 query categories
All 5 queries run via run_query() against test fixture data without errors
Each query has a pytest test verifying output column names
observatory db schema --format json emits schema as JSON
observatory db schema --format markdown emits human-readable schema table
pytest tests/test_target_queries.py passes with 0 failures

name: JSONL pipeline regression guard (M6)
description: Verify all existing tests pass after all changes. Add tests/test_regression_guard.py that: (1) confirms signals.jsonl rows contain trial_id and trial_id_full as the only new fields vs pre-PRD TrialSignals keys, (2) confirms trajectory fields (tool_call_sequence, files_read_list, files_edited_list) remain inline in signals.jsonl, (3) confirms annotation_schema.json validation still works. Run the full existing test suite and confirm 0 failures.
acceptance: The existing test suite (pytest tests/) passes with 0 failures after all PRD changes
tests/test_regression_guard.py verifies trial_id and trial_id_full are the only new TrialSignals fields
tests/test_regression_guard.py verifies trajectory fields remain inline
No existing test was modified to make it pass
pytest tests/test_regression_guard.py passes with 0 failures
